message:
feat(scheduler): add cache-aware intrusive priority heap and cache-line padding utilities

Introduce two new infrastructure modules for scheduler hot-path
optimization:

1. src/runtime/scheduler/intrusive_heap.rs (606 lines) — A binary
   max-heap that stores scheduling metadata (priority, generation,
   heap position) directly in TaskRecord fields rather than in
   separate heap-allocated entries. Uses Structure-of-Arrays (SoA)
   layout where the heap Vec stores only TaskIds while priorities
   live in the task records themselves, improving cache locality
   during heap operations. Supports O(1) lookup by task ID via
   stored heap_index, O(log n) insert/remove/priority-change.

2. src/util/cache.rs (155 lines) — CachePadded<T> wrapper that
   aligns inner data to CACHE_LINE_SIZE (64 bytes) to prevent
   false sharing between per-worker state accessed by different
   threads. Re-exported as cache::{CachePadded, CACHE_LINE_SIZE}.

Supporting changes:
- src/record/task.rs: add heap_index, sched_priority, and
  sched_generation fields to TaskRecord for intrusive heap metadata
- src/runtime/scheduler/mod.rs: declare and re-export intrusive_heap
- src/util/mod.rs: declare and re-export cache module

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
